issues.title: EXP-450 P2: EM-incorrect 1/r decay claim in 2.14 ring-magnet subsection
issues.description: The proposed 2.14 ring-magnet subsection third paragraph asserts "1/r-type decay (or 1/r² in spherically symmetric cases)" — this is wrong. Magnetic dipole far-field decays as 1/r³. Dome defenders can use this to discredit the entire proposal. Fix: delete the third paragraph entirely; paragraphs 1-2 stand on their own.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete P2 third paragraph ("The exponential form is also incompatible..."). The flux-conservation argument in paragraphs 1-2 is the strongest version of WIN-053. If a decay argument is desired, use 1/r³ dipole far-field.
issues.routing_reason: BAU priority3b: major/critical severity routed to analyst for EXP authoring

issues.title: EXP-450 P6: "in Part 2" count scope mismatch — self-contradictions not all in Part 2
issues.description: P6 proposed insertion reads "any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model..." but {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} resolves to 20 (all review self-contradictions), while only a subset are in Part 2. Fix: change "in Part 2" to "identified in this review".
issues.suggested_fix: Change P6 insertion to: "any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions identified in this review alone falsifies the model..." — scope now matches the data.
issues.routing_reason: BAU priority3b: major/critical severity routed to analyst for EXP authoring

issues.title: EXP-450 P1: 2.13 κ divide-by-zero metaphor is literal, not pedagogical
issues.description: P1 2.13 subsection headline uses "treating it as 1.67 nT/µGal is treating 1/0 as a finite number" — this is a compression metaphor that dome defenders can attack as technically inaccurate. The actual argument is that the coupling constant's denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision data.
issues.suggested_fix: Soften to: "...A coupling constant whose denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision confirmations cannot be treated as a fixed structural coefficient at the precision the dome itself cites." Remove "treating 1/0 as a finite number" framing.

issues.title: EXP-451 P2: 22.6 Hz worked example misattributes source — should be 8.77 Hz pole-floor or name averaging explicitly
issues.description: P2 worked-example says dome predicts 22.6 Hz from "flat-plate resonator with published exponentially-decaying firmament profile." But existing review (line 1063) attributes 22.6 Hz to the linear radial average H_eff = H₀·(L/R)·(1−exp(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km — an averaging convention. H(0) = 8,537 km gives 8.77 Hz (the unambiguous pole-floor). Using 22.6 Hz without naming the averaging convention mirrors the dome's own formula-switching behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Option A (cleaner): use H(0) = 8,537 km → f = c/(4H) = 8.77 Hz, no averaging needed. Option B: keep 22.6 Hz but explicitly name the integration scheme: "the linear-radial-averaged effective height H_eff ≈ 3,316 km in the quarter-wave formula".

issues.title: WIN-023: Add t=0 anchor looseness clause to defend against "5% miss" objection
issues.description: WIN-023 verdict dismisses theological link as unfalsifiable but does not say WHY the 1000-year target band is loose. A close reader could counter "Revelation 20:7 says 1000 years and SAA formed at 950 AD — that is a 5% miss, why not impressive?" The answer (t=0 has multiple candidate starts — resurrection AD33, Constantine AD312, Theodosius AD380 → band AD1033-1380, ~350yr wide) is not stated. Adding one sentence hardens the unfalsifiability verdict.
issues.routing_reason: Argument hardening for WIN-023 verdict requires scientific judgment about why 1000-year band is loose. Cannot narrow-patch without risking oversimplification.

issues.title: WIN-003: detail_evidence still asserts acoustic eigenmodes from chamber dimensions (c2 H1 carry-over)
issues.description: CARRY-OVER from c2 H1, still open in c3: detail_evidence asserts acoustic eigenmodes are determined by chamber/sarcophagus dimensions, but does not account for the dome author's specific counter that the dome's model predicts resonance from the firmament cavity geometry, not the room. The carry-over means c2's recommendation was not applied.
issues.routing_reason: Carry-over from c2/c3: substantive argument about firmament cavity geometry vs room dimensions for acoustic eigenmodes. Requires scientific analysis beyond narrow patch.

issues.title: WIN-066: Counter (1) defender-pivot appeals to globe-physics mechanism without stating why it applies (c5 H1 carry-forward)
issues.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #1: Counter (1) of the defender-pivot paragraph appeals to globe-physics mechanisms without providing the specific mechanical link that explains why globe physics produces the observed pattern. The defense survives without this but is weaker than necessary.
issues.routing_reason: WIN-066 carry-forward: Counter (1) needs specific mechanical link for globe physics producing observed pattern. Requires scientific specificity.

issues.title: EXP-462 c1 H1: §6.14 cross-reference missing KAPPA-squared finding
issues.description: EXP-462 consolidation paragraph cross-references §6.14 for KAPPA-squared and inject_ai_layer.py-PRED-001 findings, but §6.14 does NOT contain these findings. Currently in removed L3a #2 paragraph only. Either (a) preserve ~50 words naming KAPPA-squared inline in §8b.3, or (b) coordinate P2 patch adding these findings to §4.2.5/§6.14 before removing L3a.
issues.location: data/sections.json: SEC-part8b section L3a / EXP-462 P0
issues.suggested_fix: Option A: add one sentence to P0 consolidated paragraph naming KAPPA-squared (−10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.2 nT) and inject_ai_layer.py-PRED-001 findings. Option B: coordinate P2 targeting §4.2.5 or §6.14 before P0 lands.
issues.routing_reason: BAU triage: EXP-462 c1 H1 major — §6.14 cross-reference missing KAPPA-squared finding; requires analyst to either add inline naming to P0 consolidated paragraph or coordinate P2 targeting §4.2.5/§6.14 before P0 lands

issues.title: EXP-481: test_recommendations item 1 regression test lacks expected whitelist count
issues.description: test_recommendations item 1 says "Grep \b69\b in rendered docs/index.html post-build; assert match count matches the explicit whitelist" but does not supply the expected count number (~16-18 post-patches). The pseudocode leaves expected_whitelist_count as a TODO. The decider/engineer implementing this test must compute and enshrine the count independently.
issues.routing_reason: Analyst should determine whether to add a test assertion for the Grep- 69  count in docs/index.html and what the correct expected count is post-EXP-481. class_hint=verification.

issues.title: EXP-481: holes_NOT_addressed_here item 4 undercounts generate-html.js bare-69 sites (4 sites, not 2)
issues.description: EXP-481 cites 2 bare-69 sites in generate-html.js Timestamp Error tab (ts-april-2026-update). Independent scan finds 4 distinct sites: (i) line 1981 TLDR, (ii) line 1987 detail-body (two instances), (iii) line 1995 closing, (iv) line 2001 related-callout. The next EXP picking up this scope should target 4 sites, not 2.
issues.escalation_reason: Targets generate-html.js (NEVER_PUSH per routing-matrix.md). Requires human to audit and fix 4 bare-69 sites in infrastructure file. ISS-2419/2418 fixed data files; generate-html.js still needs review.

issues.title: EXP-481: part4 three-class breakdown deferral weak — 2/3 plumbing already exists
issues.description: mentions_left_unchanged item 2 defers the part4 "9 of 83 registry items (69 confirmed, 4 refined, 10 prospective)" three-class breakdown. But dome_claimed_prospective_count=10 is already in uncounted-failures.json and domeProspectiveCount is already in generate-html.js context. After EXP-481 adds dome_claimed_confirmed, only dome_claimed_refined is missing. The deferral leaves 1 site bare when 2-line change closes it. Defensible scope discipline but the asymmetry argument is underdeveloped.
issues.routing_reason: Analyst should evaluate whether the part4 "9 of 83 registry items (69 confirmed, 4 refined, 10 prospective)" prose can be templated using dome_claimed_confirmed and dome_claimed_prospective_count from uncounted-failures.json. class_hint=verification.

issues.title: SEC-8b.3 c9 H3: monitor.py-outlier steel-man kernel lost in EXP-462 P0 consolidation
issues.description: EXP-462 P0 consolidation removes the steel-man narrowing from PRE L3a paragraph 2: "monitor.py is the outlier; inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 is a BOU-family variant rather than a third independent dome formula." This concedes a legitimate dome-defender narrowing and prevents the over-counting objection. Without it, the three-formula heterogeneity claim is weaker against a defender who argues PRED-001 is not independent. Restore as one parenthetical in the §8b.3 consolidated paragraph.
issues.suggested_fix: Add parenthetical after "monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity": "(monitor.py is the outlier; inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 is a BOU-family variant rather than a third independent formula)" — or place in <details> body. ~25 words.

issues.title: SEC-8b.3 c9 H4: INTERMAGNET/Besselian-element/FSF globe-infrastructure labels removed by EXP-462 P0
issues.description: EXP-462 P0 removes the specific globe-infrastructure labels that appeared in PRE L3a paragraph 1: "BOU and W004 baselines are INTERMAGNET globe-measured observations, coverage fractions are Besselian-element computations that assume a spherical Earth, and the FSF requires per-station radial pressure integration only available under globe geometry." These labels pre-emptively answer "why these data sources depend on the globe model." Without them, a dome defender can dispute the globe-dependency of the BOU baseline. Optional (minor): restore in WIN-025 detail_evidence or §8b.3 <details> body.
issues.location: data/sections.json: SEC-part8b / EXP-462 P0 consolidation; candidate restore: data/wins.json WIN-025 detail_evidence
issues.suggested_fix: Optional: add one-line technical-label restatement to WIN-025 detail_evidence: note BOU/W004 as INTERMAGNET observations and coverage fraction as a Besselian-element computation. Lower priority than ISS-2416.

issues.title: §6.14 pointer: "geometry weight" should align with "FSF" used in §4.2.5
issues.description: §6.14 pointer characterizes §4.2.5 decomposition as "(baseline × coverage × geometry weight)" but §4.2.5 itself writes ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, where FSF is explicitly named the Footprint-Scaling Factor. "Geometry weight" is an acceptable plain-English gloss but a precise reader following the cross-reference will see "FSF" not "geometry weight". Either change pointer to use "FSF" to match the destination term, or add a parenthetical in §4.2.5 such as "(FSF, the geometry weight)". Smaller edit: change the pointer.
issues.suggested_fix: In part6.html pointer, replace "(baseline × coverage × geometry weight)" with "(baseline × coverage × FSF)" to match §4.2.5 term usage.

issues.title: sections.json part1.html uses hardcoded #fafafa fallback in CSS variable
issues.description: sections.json part1.html contains background:var(--surface-1,#fafafa) — #fafafa is a light-theme color as a CSS variable fallback. Only triggers if --surface-1 is undefined. Not an immediate dark-theme break but a latent light-theme leak.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the fallback or use a neutral/transparent fallback: background:var(--surface-1)

issues.title: 3 template placeholders literal in published HTML: {{V51_NO_MONITOR}}, {{V51_POST_HOC}}, {{V51_NO_DERIVE}}
issues.description: data/sections.json (part2.html) uses {{V51_NO_MONITOR}}, {{V51_POST_HOC}}, {{V51_NO_DERIVE}} but generate-html.js substitution map is missing these. Computed values: V51_NO_MONITOR=27, V51_POST_HOC=31, V51_NO_DERIVE=31 (out of 32 new_in_v51 WINs). Readers see literal {{...}} text in docs/index.html. Fix: add to generate-html.js substitution map. Route to tinker (NEVER_PUSH constraint on build scripts).
issues.escalation_reason: Fix requires editing build-scripts/generate-html.js which is NEVER_PUSH (classified git, cannot be edited from FUSE workspace). Human or tinker must make the change from a clone.

issues.title: audit-exp-tracker-gaps.js: missing archive JSONL read — does not ingest expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl
issues.description: PROP-053 audit script does not read monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl (423 entries, 372 distinct IDs under PROP-022 phase 4). EXPs in archive are mis-counted as "missing" — 380 phantom orphan_file gaps reported. Fix: add archive ingestion; change orphan check to require EXP absent from BOTH live items[] AND archive. Curmudgeon review: audit-exp-tracker-gaps.c1.json (critical).
issues.escalation_reason: Pipeline infrastructure fix (PROP-053 audit script + reference doc). Tinker is primary owner. Curmudgeon review audit-exp-tracker-gaps.c1.json documents specific fixes needed.

issues.title: audit-exp-tracker-gaps.js: missing no_trace category (PROP-053 spec)
issues.description: PROP-053 specified four categories: orphan_file, tracker_referenced_no_file, mentioned_only, no_trace. Script does not implement no_trace (originally 41 of 94 missing IDs, pure phantom bulk-reservations). Fix: iterate EXP-001 to next_id-1, classify by (files, live, archive, mentions) membership, output no_trace bucket. Update PROP-053 implementation_note to match shipped output schema.
issues.escalation_reason: Pipeline infrastructure fix (PROP-053 audit script + reference doc). Tinker is primary owner. Curmudgeon review audit-exp-tracker-gaps.c1.json documents specific fixes needed.

issues.title: expansion-tracker-gap-semantics.md: canonizes archive-blind bug as correct behavior
issues.description: Reference doc says "EXP-001 through ~EXP-450 = pre-tracker, benign" — there is no pre-tracker era. The tracker has always existed; PROP-022 phase 4 split it into live+archive. A real orphan_file is a file on disk absent from BOTH live items[] AND archive JSONL regardless of EXP number. Rewrite reference doc to reflect actual architecture. Curmudgeon review: audit-exp-tracker-gaps.c1.json (major).
issues.escalation_reason: Pipeline infrastructure fix (PROP-053 audit script + reference doc). Tinker is primary owner. Curmudgeon review audit-exp-tracker-gaps.c1.json documents specific fixes needed.

issues.description: PROP-053 audit script output schema does not match spec. Spec requires top-level fields: total_slots, present, missing_total, missing_ranges, missing_in_context. Script ships different fields. Spec/code/doc trio disagrees — must pick one direction and align all three.

issues.description: PROP-053 implementation_note in the spec misdescribes the shipped script: claims --summary and --json-output flags exist (they do not), and misses the archive-blind behavior as a documented limitation. Spec is misleading to future maintainers.
issues.title: PROP-053: implementation_note misdescribes shipped script (missing flags, silent archive-blind)

issues.description: PROP-053 audit script cross-reference scan only walks monitor/analyst/expansions/. Spec requires also scanning monitor/decisions/ and monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ for EXP-NNN mentions. mentionedIds set is incomplete.

issues.description: PROP-053 listed three deliverables: script, reference doc, and structure-integrity.md edit (Section 4 of ref doc describes the edit: report MODERATE when orphan_file gaps > 5, otherwise INFO). Only two shipped. integrity check does not flag orphan gaps.
